easy: Ace: to excel at something; also a playing card or tennis serve.
Eat: to consume food; to corrode or wear away.
Top: the highest point; to surpass or exceed.
Ran: past tense of run; moved swiftly on foot.
Cat: a small domesticated carnivorous mammal.
Tap: to strike lightly; a device controlling flow.
Oar: a pole used to row a boat.
Elm: a tall deciduous tree with serrated leaves.
Dam: a barrier built across a river; to block.
Net: a woven mesh; a score in tennis.
Dog: a domesticated carnivore kept as a pet.
But: except; however; a conjunction of contrast.
Sap: the fluid of a plant; to drain energy.
Pan: a flat cooking vessel; to criticize harshly.
Car: a wheeled motor vehicle for transport.
Now: at the present time; immediately.
Rats: small rodents; also slang for betrayers.
Step: a movement of the foot; a stage in a process.
Wolf: a wild carnivorous mammal; to eat greedily.
Able: having the power or skill to do something.
West: the direction of the setting sun.
Lake: a large body of water surrounded by land.
Care: concern for someone; to look after.
King: a male monarch; the most powerful chess piece.
Lump: a compact mass; to group together.
Mist: thin fog; a fine spray of liquid.
Past: gone by in time; a previous period.
Pool: a small body of water; a shared resource.
Time: the progression of events; to measure duration.
Tips: the pointed ends; helpful pieces of advice.
Fool: a person lacking sense; to trick someone.
Bale: a large bound bundle of hay or cloth.
Snap: to break sharply; a quick fastening device.
Tams: beret-like caps originating in Scotland.
Duel: a formal combat between two people.
Rode: past tense of ride; traveled on horseback.
medium: Croak: the sound a frog makes; to die (slang).
Store: a place to buy goods; to keep for future use.
Chase: to pursue; an act of running after someone.
Thick: having a large distance between surfaces; dense.
Place: a particular area; to put something somewhere.
Range: the area covered; a series between limits.
Right: correct; the direction opposite to left.
Clock: a device that measures time.
Spare: to refrain from harming; extra, not used.
Trick: a clever act; to deceive someone.
Mates: companions or friends; checkmates in chess.
Sharp: having a fine edge; mentally quick.
Reign: the period of a monarch's rule; to hold power.
Bench: a long seat; a judge's seat in court.
Table: a flat surface on legs; to postpone a motion.
Pitch: the degree of highness of sound; a playing field.
Trial: a formal legal examination; a test of quality.
Round: circular in shape; a sequence or series.
Laugh: to make sounds expressing amusement.
Fleat: to skin or flay; a variant form of fleece.
Sleep: a natural resting state; to be dormant.
Lofts: upper floors or attic spaces; to hit high.
Limit: a point beyond which something cannot proceed.
Lands: areas of ground; to arrive by aircraft or boat.
Melts: becomes liquid due to heat; softens emotionally.
Winds: moving air; to turn or coil around something.
Burns: injuries from fire; a Scottish stream.
Tores: large convex moldings in architecture.
Glare: a fierce or angry stare; harsh bright light.
Match: a contest; a thin stick for lighting fire.
hard: Plates: flat dishes; tectonic slabs of Earth's crust.
Retain: to keep possession of; to remember.
Master: an expert; to acquire complete skill in.
Puzzle: a problem for creative thinking; to confuse.
Plants: living organisms that photosynthesize; factories.
Stamps: marks made by pressing; postage labels.
Garble: to mix up a message until it is confused.
Makers: people who create or manufacture things.
Bloats: swells with liquid or gas; inflates abnormally.
Crafts: skilled handwork; to make with skill.
Snorts: makes a sharp sound through the nose.
Planet: a celestial body orbiting a star.
Tables: flat surfaces on legs; lists of data.
Carton: a lightweight box made of cardboard.
Garden: a plot for growing plants; to tend plants.
Haunta: places frequently visited; to repeatedly trouble.
Flared: spread outward; burned with sudden brightness.
Forest: a large area covered chiefly with trees.
Closer: nearer in distance; more intimate.
Slated: scheduled; covered with slate tiles.
Dreamt: past tense of dream; experienced visions in sleep.
Stipel: a small leaflike part at base of a leaflet.
Battle: a fight between armies; to struggle vigorously.
Claret: a type of dry red Bordeaux wine.
Rondeau: a short poem of fixed form with a refrain.
Giving: the act of donating; generous in nature.
Forces: physical powers; military units; to compel.
Stable: firm; a building for horses; not changing.
Angler: a person who fishes with a hook and line.
Gnarled: knobbly and twisted, especially with age.
expert: Compute: to calculate or process mathematically.
Granted: given officially; acknowledged as true.
Eclipse: the obscuring of light by another body.
Tangelo: a citrus fruit — a cross between tangerine and pomelo.
Flatrop: a vessel with a flat upper deck.
Magnets: objects that attract iron; things with powerful appeal.
Blotchy: covered with irregular patches of color.
Trampled: walked heavily over; crushed underfoot.
Picture: a painting, drawing, or photograph; to visualize.
Gnarled: twisted and knobbly; worn with age.
Topical: relating to current events; applied to a body part.
Balcony: a platform projecting from a building's wall.
Compute: to reckon or calculate a result.
Catnaps: short sleeps; to sleep briefly.
Raspers: things that scrape or grate roughly.
Plaster: a paste hardening when dry; to cover a surface.
Planets: large spherical bodies orbiting a star.
Wasters: people who waste resources; spoiled pieces.
Eructs: belches; emits from within suddenly.
Comets: icy space bodies with bright tails near the sun.
Terming: naming or calling something; setting a term.
Caplets: small tablet-shaped capsules of medicine.
Patrism: devotion to patriarchal authority systems.
Plastered: covered with plaster; very drunk (informal).
Tangles: twisted knots; to become entangled.
Tranced: put into a state of trance.
Cluster: a group of similar things close together.
Centers: middle points; main institutions of activity.
Spatula: a broad flat bladed utensil for mixing.
Sprinted: ran at full speed over a short distance.
Clambers: climbs awkwardly using hands and feet.
Stackpile: an accumulated pile of stored goods.
Prancest: moved by springing forward on hind legs.
Stampede: a sudden rush of frightened animals or people.
Cornplace: a market or trading location for grain.
Carpenter: a person who makes and repairs wooden objects.
